question: What's the actual monthly price for Housecall Pro's Basic plan, and what happens if I add a second user?
key_facts: Basic plan is $79/mo billed monthly or $59/mo billed annually.
Basic plan is capped at 1 user.
Adding a second user forces an upgrade to Essentials, which is $149/mo billed annually.

question: How is Jobber's AI Receptionist add-on priced now, and how did that change recently?
key_facts: It was repriced from a flat $99/mo to a usage-based $29/mo including 30 conversations.
Additional conversations beyond the 30 included cost $0.79 each.
It remains included at no extra cost, with unlimited usage, on the Plus plan.
The change was first detected/logged on 2026-07-02.

question: What actually makes ServiceTitan's Dispatch Pro different from a GPS-heuristic scheduling tool?
key_facts: Dispatch Pro uses machine learning to predict job value and technician performance.
It recommends/auto-assigns technicians to maximize captured revenue, not just minimize drive time.
It is GA and sold as a paid Pro add-on with no public price.

question: Does Housecall Pro support custom fields on customer records?
key_facts: Housecall Pro's help center explicitly states custom fields are not supported anywhere, including on customer profiles.
Users are told to 'vote' for the feature via support chat rather than being given a timeline.

question: Is Jobber's in-app AI copilot generally available or still in beta as of mid-2026?
key_facts: Jobber's copilot (Jobber AI Voice and Chat) is still officially labeled Beta in Jobber's help center as of mid-2026.
It is free to all US/Canada customers.
It launched Oct 1, 2024 as 'Jobber Copilot.'

question: When did ServiceTitan's AP Automation (invoice OCR capture) actually go GA, and what does it do?
key_facts: AP Automation was announced/GA on Jan 8, 2026.
It uses Titan Intelligence OCR to auto-fill vendor invoice fields from a scanned inbox.
It performs three-way matching against POs and receipts.
In-app bill payment was separately slated for a Spring 2026 rollout, not yet confirmed live.

question: What's required before Jobber will auto-draft quotes on every new request without manual triggering?
key_facts: Enabling Automatic Draft Quotes requires at least 3 quotes previously sent via 'Draft for me' so the model has examples.
'Draft for me' builds quotes with suggested line items from request details, saved templates, and past quotes.

question: Can Housecall Pro's CSR AI handle text messages today, or only calls and web chat?
key_facts: CSR AI answers calls and website chats 24/7.
Native SMS handling for CSR AI is listed as 'coming soon' per the help center, not yet shipped.

question: What card and ACH processing rates does ServiceTitan publish for its payments product?
key_facts: Published card processing rate is 2.9% + $0.30 (Stripe-based).
ACH rate is 0.8%.
ServiceTitan also integrates TSYS, NMI, and Adyen with negotiable interchange-plus pricing at scale.

question: How much does the Profit Rhino managed price-book content cost inside Housecall Pro, and which trades does it cover?
key_facts: Profit Rhino costs roughly $199/month flat regardless of how many trade books are loaded.
Coverage spans HVAC, Plumbing, Drain Cleaning, and Electrical.
Content updates land quarterly.

question: What does Jobber charge for Instant Payout and how fast is the deposit?
key_facts: Jobber's Instant Payout costs a 1% fee.
Funds land within seconds, including weekends and holidays, skipping the standard ~2-day window.

question: Is ServiceTitan's 'Atlas in the Office' agentic feature set actually usable by customers yet?
key_facts: The four Atlas office modules entered private preview in the Fall 2025 release, gated to select accounts via waitlist.
A May 28, 2026 customer success story shows Atlas building Adaptive Capacity booking rules in ~30 minutes vs. an estimated two weeks manually.
ServiceTitan's own marketing page still labels this 'Coming soon,' which is stale versus the private-preview reality.

question: Is ServiceTitan's AI lead in dispatch matched by an equivalent lead in AI marketing content generation?
key_facts: ServiceTitan is the clear market-leading vendor in ML-driven dispatch via Dispatch Pro.
In generative marketing content, ServiceTitan trails Housecall Pro and Jobber because its Email Content Generator and Audience Assistant are still 'Coming soon.'
No vendor is rated market-leading on generative marketing content.

question: Between Jobber's and Housecall Pro's AI phone receptionists, which is priced more transparently?
key_facts: Jobber's AI Receptionist is vendor-confirmed at $29/mo including 30 conversations, $0.79 per extra conversation.
Housecall Pro's CSR AI voice add-on has no public price; it's listed as 'contact for pricing.'
HCP markets CSR AI with a claimed 42x ROI instead of a disclosed price.
Jobber's Plus plan (roughly $449-599/mo annual) includes AI Receptionist with unlimited usage free.

question: What unresolved verification issues exist around ServiceTitan's agentic office features and Housecall Pro's CSR AI pricing?
key_facts: ServiceTitan's agentic-workflow offering is flagged needs_verification because its autonomy claim rests on a single public customer story with limited access.
Housecall Pro's customer-contact-agent offering is flagged needs_verification because CSR AI's exact price isn't public.
Contractor reports suggest CSR AI costs roughly $200-500+/mo, unconfirmed by the vendor.

question: Does Housecall Pro's 'Write it for me' actually build a quote's line items the way Jobber's 'Draft for me' does?
key_facts: Jobber's 'Draft for me' generates a quote including suggested line items from the request, templates, and past quotes.
Housecall Pro's 'Write it for me' only drafts a text summary from line items the user has already entered.
Neither vendor does invoice/receipt OCR or photo-to-quote.

question: As of mid-2026, is Jobber or ServiceTitan further ahead on AI back-office automation?
key_facts: Both are rated depth 'strong' for back-office automation as of their 2026 as_of dates.
Jobber's strength is generative quote construction via 'Draft for me.'
ServiceTitan's strength is AP Automation (OCR capture + 3-way matching), GA since Jan 8, 2026.
Neither is rated market-leading; no vendor has shipped the deeper document/data automation needed for that rating.

question: How does ServiceTitan's Pricebook Pro compare to Jobber's product list in managed content and estimated cost?
key_facts: Jobber's catalog is a flat Products & Services list with no managed/pre-built trade content, only a Home Depot in-quote lookup.
ServiceTitan's Pricebook Pro is a paid add-on offering pre-built multi-trade content curated via Titan Intelligence's Smart Start.
Pricebook Pro is reported (unverified by vendor) at roughly $200-500/month, not publicly listed by ServiceTitan.

question: Since all three vendors now draft AI review responses, what actually separates ServiceTitan's reputation tools from the other two?
key_facts: All three vendors ship AI-drafted review responses, making this a mature, even field.
ServiceTitan monitors 60+ listing sites with review-to-technician/customer/job attribution.
Housecall Pro monitors only Google/Facebook and Jobber monitors only Google Business Profile.
ServiceTitan's Review Response Generator adds named tone controls (Professional/Casual/Enthusiastic), length options, and a custom-instruction field.

question: Is two-way SMS messaging free on all Jobber plans, and how does that line up with the Grow tier's published price?
key_facts: Two-way SMS messaging in Jobber is gated to the Grow plan and up, per the CRM/communications teardown.
Jobber's Grow plan (Individual) lists at $149/mo billed annually per the pricing profile.
Housecall Pro's SMS-capable Chat inbox and Texting Number are not called out as similarly plan-gated.

question: Across all 7 AI capability areas, in which ones is ServiceTitan actually rated 'market-leading' rather than just 'strong'?
key_facts: ServiceTitan is rated depth=market_leading in customer-contact-agent (AI Voice Agent + Contact Center Pro).
ServiceTitan is rated depth=market_leading in dispatch-scheduling (Dispatch Pro ML engine).
ServiceTitan's back-office-automation, marketing-content, reputation-reviews, in-product-copilot, and agentic-workflow offerings are all rated below market_leading (strong, basic, or beta).

question: Which competitor leads in each of FieldPulse's seven tracked AI capability areas?
key_facts: ServiceTitan leads customer-contact-agent and is the sole market-leading vendor in dispatch-scheduling.
Housecall Pro leads in-product-copilot with the broadest free, default-on GA copilots (Analyst AI, Coach AI).
Jobber leads back-office-automation on the single most substantive capability, generative quote drafting.
No vendor is rated market-leading in marketing-content, reputation-reviews, back-office-automation, or agentic-workflow.

question: How many of the three FSM competitors ship true ML-based dispatch today, and what do the other two use instead?
key_facts: Only ServiceTitan ships true ML-driven dispatch (Dispatch Pro).
Jobber uses heuristic route optimization (drive time, traffic, road closures) with no ML technician assignment.
Housecall Pro has no native route optimization at all, relying on the third-party Beeline Routes integration.
Both Jobber and Housecall Pro are rated status 'none' on AI dispatch as defined.

question: Has any competitor actually shipped fully autonomous, end-to-end office workflow agents yet, or is it all roadmap?
key_facts: No FSM vendor has shipped true multi-step autonomous agents running entire office workflows end-to-end as of mid-2026.
ServiceTitan's 'Atlas in the Office' agentic modules are in private preview to select accounts, not broad GA.
Housecall Pro's CSR AI is genuinely autonomous but narrow, limited to single-domain call booking.
Jobber runs rule/trigger-based automations plus AI-drafted quotes with human oversight, not goal-seeking agents.

question: List each vendor's AI voice/receptionist pricing as of July 2026, and flag which figures are vendor-confirmed vs. just reported.
key_facts: Jobber AI Receptionist: $29/mo incl. 30 conversations, $0.79/extra conversation, vendor-confirmed on getjobber.com/pricing.
Housecall Pro CSR AI: price not publicly disclosed, listed as 'contact for pricing.'
ServiceTitan AI Voice Agent: usage-based per-call fee, exact rate not publicly disclosed anywhere.

question: How many of the three competitors offer native custom fields on customer records, and how do their implementations differ?
key_facts: Jobber offers 6 custom field types, scoped to client/property/quote/job/invoice/team, some client-facing and transferable.
ServiceTitan offers 3 custom field types across 8 record types, including surfacing on the Call Booking screen.
Housecall Pro has no custom fields at all — explicitly unsupported per its help center.

question: How many employees does Housecall Pro currently have?
What's on FieldPulse's own internal AI product roadmap for the next two quarters?
How does Successware's AI dispatch feature compare to ServiceTitan's Dispatch Pro?
What was ServiceTitan's total company revenue for fiscal year 2026?
